root_text.an3.100:1.2: ‘yathā yathāyaṁ puriso kammaṁ karoti tathā tathā taṁ paṭisaṁvediyatī’ti, evaṁ santaṁ, bhikkhave, brahmacariyavāso na hoti, okāso na paññāyati sammā dukkhassa antakiriyāya.
root_text.an3.100:1.3: Yo ca kho, bhikkhave, evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.an3.100:1.4: ‘yathā yathā vedanīyaṁ ayaṁ puriso kammaṁ karoti tathā tathāssa vipākaṁ paṭisaṁvediyatī’ti, evaṁ santaṁ, bhikkhave, brahmacariyavāso hoti, okāso paññāyati sammā dukkhassa antakiriyāya.
root_text.an3.100:1.5: Idha, bhikkhave, ekaccassa puggalassa appamattakampi pāpakammaṁ kataṁ tamenaṁ nirayaṁ upaneti.
root_text.an3.100:1.6: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekaccassa puggalassa tādisaṁyeva appamattakaṁ pāpakammaṁ kataṁ diṭṭhadhammavedanīyaṁ hoti, nāṇupi khāyati, kiṁ bahudeva.
root_text.an3.100:2.1: Kathaṁrūpassa, bhikkhave, puggalassa appamattakampi pāpakammaṁ kataṁ tamenaṁ nirayaṁ upaneti?
root_text.an3.100:2.2: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo abhāvitakāyo hoti abhāvitasīlo abhāvitacitto abhāvitapañño paritto appātumo appadukkhavihārī.
root_text.an3.100:2.3: Evarūpassa, bhikkhave, puggalassa appamattakampi pāpakammaṁ kataṁ tamenaṁ nirayaṁ upaneti.
root_text.an3.100:3.1: Kathaṁrūpassa, bhikkhave, puggalassa tādisaṁyeva appamattakaṁ pāpakammaṁ kataṁ diṭṭhadhammavedanīyaṁ hoti, nāṇupi khāyati, kiṁ bahudeva?
root_text.an3.100:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo bhāvitakāyo hoti bhāvitasīlo bhāvitacitto bhāvitapañño aparitto mahatto appamāṇavihārī.
root_text.an3.100:3.3: Evarūpassa, bhikkhave, puggalassa tādisaṁyeva appamattakaṁ pāpakammaṁ kataṁ diṭṭhadhammavedanīyaṁ hoti, nāṇupi khāyati, kiṁ bahudeva.
root_text.an3.100:4.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, puriso loṇakapallaṁ paritte udakamallake pakkhipeyya.
root_text.an3.100:4.3: api nu taṁ parittaṁ udakaṁ amunā loṇakapallena loṇaṁ assa apeyyan”ti?
root_text.an3.100:4.6: “Aduñhi, bhante, parittaṁ udakakapallake udakaṁ, taṁ amunā loṇakapallena loṇaṁ assa apeyyan”ti.
root_text.an3.100:4.7: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, puriso loṇakapallakaṁ gaṅgāya nadiyā pakkhipeyya.
root_text.an3.100:4.9: api nu sā gaṅgā nadī amunā loṇakapallena loṇaṁ assa apeyyā”ti?
root_text.an3.100:4.12: “Asu hi, bhante, gaṅgāya nadiyā mahā udakakkhandho so amunā loṇakapallena loṇo na assa apeyyo”ti.
root_text.an3.100:5.1: “Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, idhekaccassa puggalassa appamattakampi pāpakammaṁ kataṁ tamenaṁ nirayaṁ upaneti.
root_text.an3.100:5.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekaccassa puggalassa tādisaṁyeva appamattakaṁ pāpakammaṁ kataṁ diṭṭhadhammavedanīyaṁ hoti, nāṇupi khāyati, kiṁ bahudeva.
root_text.an3.100:6.1: Kathaṁrūpassa, bhikkhave, puggalassa appamattakampi pāpakammaṁ kataṁ tamenaṁ nirayaṁ upaneti?
root_text.an3.100:6.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo abhāvitakāyo hoti abhāvitasīlo abhāvitacitto abhāvitapañño paritto appātumo appadukkhavihārī.
root_text.an3.100:6.3: Evarūpassa, bhikkhave, puggalassa appamattakampi pāpakammaṁ kataṁ tamenaṁ nirayaṁ upaneti.
root_text.an3.100:7.1: Kathaṁrūpassa, bhikkhave, puggalassa tādisaṁyeva appamattakaṁ pāpakammaṁ kataṁ diṭṭhadhammavedanīyaṁ hoti, nāṇupi khāyati, kiṁ bahudeva?
root_text.an3.100:7.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo bhāvitakāyo hoti bhāvitasīlo bhāvitacitto bhāvitapañño aparitto mahatto appamāṇavihārī.
root_text.an3.100:7.3: Evarūpassa, bhikkhave, puggalassa tādisaṁyeva appamattakaṁ pāpakammaṁ kataṁ diṭṭhadhammavedanīyaṁ hoti, nāṇupi khāyati, kiṁ bahudeva.
root_text.an3.100:8.1: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco aḍḍhakahāpaṇenapi bandhanaṁ nigacchati, kahāpaṇenapi bandhanaṁ nigacchati, kahāpaṇasatenapi bandhanaṁ nigacchati.
root_text.an3.100:8.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco aḍḍhakahāpaṇenapi na bandhanaṁ nigacchati, kahāpaṇenapi na bandhanaṁ nigacchati, kahāpaṇasatenapi na bandhanaṁ nigacchati.
root_text.an3.100:9.1: Kathaṁrūpo, bhikkhave, aḍḍhakahāpaṇenapi bandhanaṁ nigacchati, kahāpaṇenapi bandhanaṁ nigacchati, kahāpaṇasatenapi bandhanaṁ nigacchati?
root_text.an3.100:9.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco daliddo hoti appassako appabhogo.
root_text.an3.100:9.3: Evarūpo, bhikkhave, aḍḍhakahāpaṇenapi bandhanaṁ nigacchati, kahāpaṇenapi bandhanaṁ nigacchati, kahāpaṇasatenapi bandhanaṁ nigacchati.
root_text.an3.100:10.1: Kathaṁrūpo, bhikkhave, aḍḍhakahāpaṇenapi na bandhanaṁ nigacchati, kahāpaṇenapi na bandhanaṁ nigacchati, kahāpaṇasatenapi na bandhanaṁ nigacchati?
root_text.an3.100:10.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco aḍḍho hoti mahaddhano mahābhogo.
root_text.an3.100:10.3: Evarūpo, bhikkhave, aḍḍhakahāpaṇenapi na bandhanaṁ nigacchati, kahāpaṇenapi na bandhanaṁ nigacchati, kahāpaṇasatenapi na bandhanaṁ nigacchati.
root_text.an3.100:10.4: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, idhekaccassa puggalassa appamattakaṁ pāpakammaṁ kataṁ.
root_text.an3.100:10.6: Idha, bhikkhave, ekaccassa puggalassa tādisaṁyeva appamattakaṁ pāpakammaṁ kataṁ diṭṭhadhammavedanīyaṁ hoti, nāṇupi khāyati, kiṁ bahudeva.
root_text.an3.100:11.1: Kathaṁrūpassa, bhikkhave, puggalassa appamattakaṁ pāpakammaṁ kataṁ, tamenaṁ nirayaṁ upaneti?
root_text.an3.100:11.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo abhāvitakāyo hoti abhāvitasīlo abhāvitacitto abhāvitapañño paritto appātumo appadukkhavihārī.
root_text.an3.100:11.3: Evarūpassa, bhikkhave, puggalassa tādisaṁyeva appamattakaṁ pāpakammaṁ kataṁ tamenaṁ nirayaṁ upaneti.
root_text.an3.100:12.1: Kathaṁrūpassa, bhikkhave, puggalassa tādisaṁyeva appamattakaṁ pāpakammaṁ kataṁ diṭṭhadhammavedanīyaṁ hoti, nāṇupi khāyati, kiṁ bahudeva?
root_text.an3.100:12.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo bhāvitakāyo hoti bhāvitasīlo bhāvitacitto bhāvitapañño aparitto mahatto appamāṇavihārī.
root_text.an3.100:12.3: Evarūpassa, bhikkhave, puggalassa tādisaṁyeva appamattakaṁ pāpakammaṁ kataṁ diṭṭhadhammavedanīyaṁ hoti, nāṇupi khāyati, kiṁ bahudeva.
root_text.an3.100:13.1: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo bhāvitakāyo hoti bhāvitasīlo bhāvitacitto bhāvitapañño aparitto mahatto appamāṇavihārī.
root_text.an3.100:13.2: Evarūpassa, bhikkhave, puggalassa tādisaṁyeva appamattakaṁ pāpakammaṁ kataṁ diṭṭhadhammavedanīyaṁ hoti, nāṇupi khāyati, kiṁ bahudeva.
root_text.an3.100:13.3: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, orabbhiko vā urabbhaghātako vā appekaccaṁ urabbhaṁ adinnaṁ ādiyamānaṁ pahoti hantuṁ vā bandhituṁ vā jāpetuṁ vā yathāpaccayaṁ vā kātuṁ, appekaccaṁ urabbhaṁ adinnaṁ ādiyamānaṁ nappahoti hantuṁ vā bandhituṁ vā jāpetuṁ vā yathāpaccayaṁ vā kātuṁ.
root_text.an3.100:14.1: Kathaṁrūpaṁ, bhikkhave, orabbhiko vā urabbhaghātako vā urabbhaṁ adinnaṁ ādiyamānaṁ pahoti hantuṁ vā bandhituṁ vā jāpetuṁ vā yathāpaccayaṁ vā kātuṁ?
root_text.an3.100:14.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco daliddo hoti appassako appabhogo.
root_text.an3.100:14.3: Evarūpaṁ, bhikkhave, orabbhiko vā urabbhaghātako vā urabbhaṁ adinnaṁ ādiyamānaṁ pahoti hantuṁ vā bandhituṁ vā jāpetuṁ vā yathāpaccayaṁ vā kātuṁ.
root_text.an3.100:15.1: Kathaṁrūpaṁ, bhikkhave, orabbhiko vā urabbhaghātako vā urabbhaṁ adinnaṁ ādiyamānaṁ nappahoti hantuṁ vā bandhituṁ vā jāpetuṁ vā yathāpaccayaṁ vā kātuṁ.
root_text.an3.100:15.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco aḍḍho hoti mahaddhano mahābhogo rājā vā rājamahāmatto vā.
root_text.an3.100:15.3: Evarūpaṁ, bhikkhave, orabbhiko vā urabbhaghātako vā urabbhaṁ adinnaṁ ādiyamānaṁ nappahoti hantuṁ vā bandhituṁ vā jāpetuṁ vā yathāpaccayaṁ vā kātuṁ.
root_text.an3.100:15.5: ‘dehi me, mārisa, urabbhaṁ vā urabbhadhanaṁ vā’ti.
root_text.an3.100:15.6: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, idhekaccassa puggalassa tādisaṁyeva appamattakampi pāpakammaṁ kataṁ tamenaṁ nirayaṁ upaneti.
root_text.an3.100:15.7: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekaccassa puggalassa tādisaṁyeva appamattakaṁ pāpakammaṁ kataṁ diṭṭhadhammavedanīyaṁ hoti, nāṇupi khāyati, kiṁ bahudeva.
root_text.an3.100:16.1: Kathaṁrūpassa, bhikkhave, puggalassa appamattakampi pāpakammaṁ kataṁ tamenaṁ nirayaṁ upaneti?
root_text.an3.100:16.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo abhāvitakāyo hoti abhāvitasīlo abhāvitacitto abhāvitapañño paritto appātumo appadukkhavihārī.
root_text.an3.100:16.3: Evarūpassa, bhikkhave, puggalassa appamattakampi pāpakammaṁ kataṁ tamenaṁ nirayaṁ upaneti.
root_text.an3.100:17.1: Kathaṁrūpassa, bhikkhave, puggalassa tādisaṁyeva appamattakaṁ pāpakammaṁ kataṁ diṭṭhadhammavedanīyaṁ hoti, nāṇupi khāyati, kiṁ bahudeva?
root_text.an3.100:17.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo bhāvitakāyo hoti bhāvitasīlo bhāvitacitto bhāvitapañño aparitto mahatto appamāṇavihārī.
root_text.an3.100:17.3: Evarūpassa, bhikkhave, puggalassa tādisaṁyeva appamattakaṁ pāpakammaṁ kataṁ diṭṭhadhammavedanīyaṁ hoti, nāṇupi khāyati, kiṁ bahudeva.
root_text.an3.100:18.2: ‘yathā yathāyaṁ puriso kammaṁ karoti tathā tathā taṁ paṭisaṁvedetī’ti, evaṁ santaṁ, bhikkhave, brahmacariyavāso na hoti, okāso na paññāyati sammā dukkhassa antakiriyāya.
root_text.an3.100:18.3: Yo ca kho, bhikkhave, evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.an3.100:18.4: ‘yathā yathā vedanīyaṁ ayaṁ puriso kammaṁ karoti tathā tathā tassa vipākaṁ paṭisaṁvedetī’ti, evaṁ santaṁ, bhikkhave, brahmacariyavāso hoti, okāso paññāyati sammā dukkhassa antakiriyāyā”ti.

translation_text.an3.100:1.2: ‘No matter how this person performs a deed, they experience it the same way.’ This being so, the spiritual life could not be lived, and there’d be no chance of making a complete end of suffering.
translation_text.an3.100:1.4: ‘No matter how this person performs a deed, they experience the result as it should be experienced.’ This being so, the spiritual life can be lived, and there is a chance of making a complete end of suffering.
translation_text.an3.100:1.5: Take the case of the individual who does a trivial bad deed, but it lands them in hell.
translation_text.an3.100:1.6: Meanwhile, another individual does the same trivial bad deed, but experiences it in this life, so that not even a little remains, let alone a lot.
translation_text.an3.100:2.1: What kind of individual does a trivial bad deed, but it lands them in hell?
translation_text.an3.100:2.2: An individual who hasn’t developed their physical endurance, ethics, mind, or wisdom. They’re small-minded and mean-spirited, living in suffering.
translation_text.an3.100:2.3: That kind of individual does a trivial bad deed, but it lands them in hell.
translation_text.an3.100:3.1: What kind of individual does the same trivial bad deed, but experiences it in this life, so that not even a little remains, let alone a lot?
translation_text.an3.100:3.2: An individual who has developed their physical endurance, ethics, mind, and wisdom. They’re not small-minded, but are big-hearted, meditating on the immeasurables.
translation_text.an3.100:3.3: That kind of individual does the same trivial bad deed, but experiences it in this life, so that not even a little remains, let alone a lot.
translation_text.an3.100:4.1: Suppose a person was to drop a lump of salt into a mug of water.
translation_text.an3.100:4.3: Would that mug of water become salty and undrinkable?”
translation_text.an3.100:4.6: Because there is only a little water in the bowl.”
translation_text.an3.100:4.7: “Suppose a person was to drop a lump of salt into the Ganges river.
translation_text.an3.100:4.9: Would the Ganges river become salty and undrinkable?”
translation_text.an3.100:4.12: Because the Ganges river is a vast mass of water.”
translation_text.an3.100:5.1: “This is how it is in the case of an individual who does a trivial bad deed, but it lands them in hell.
translation_text.an3.100:5.2: Meanwhile, another individual does the same trivial bad deed, but experiences it in this life, so that not even a little remains, let alone a lot. …
translation_text.an3.100:8.1: Take the case of someone who is thrown in jail for stealing half a dollar, a dollar, or a hundred dollars.
translation_text.an3.100:8.2: While someone else isn’t thrown in jail for stealing half a dollar, a dollar, or a hundred dollars.
translation_text.an3.100:9.1: What sort of person is thrown in jail for stealing half a dollar, a dollar, or a hundred dollars?
translation_text.an3.100:9.2: Someone who is poor, with few possessions and little wealth.
translation_text.an3.100:9.3: That kind of person is thrown in jail for stealing half a dollar, a dollar, or a hundred dollars.
translation_text.an3.100:10.1: What sort of person isn’t thrown in jail for stealing half a dollar, a dollar, or a hundred dollars?
translation_text.an3.100:10.2: Someone who is rich, affluent, and wealthy.
translation_text.an3.100:10.3: That kind of person isn’t thrown in jail for stealing half a dollar, a dollar, or a hundred dollars.
translation_text.an3.100:10.4: This is how it is in the case of an individual who does a trivial bad deed, but they go to hell.
translation_text.an3.100:10.6: Meanwhile, another individual does the same trivial bad deed, but experiences it in this life, so that not even a little remains, let alone a lot. …
translation_text.an3.100:13.3: It’s like a sheep dealer or butcher. They can execute, jail, fine, or otherwise punish one person who steals from them, but not another.
translation_text.an3.100:14.1: What sort of person can they punish?
translation_text.an3.100:14.2: Someone who is poor, with few possessions and little wealth.
translation_text.an3.100:14.3: That’s the kind of person they can punish.
translation_text.an3.100:15.1: What sort of person can’t they punish?
translation_text.an3.100:15.2: A ruler or their chief minister who is rich, affluent, and wealthy.
translation_text.an3.100:15.3: That’s the kind of person they can’t punish.
translation_text.an3.100:15.4: In fact, all they can do is raise their joined palms and ask:
translation_text.an3.100:15.5: ‘Please, good fellow, give me my sheep or pay me for it.’
translation_text.an3.100:15.6: This is how it is in the case of an individual who does a trivial bad deed, but it lands them in hell.
translation_text.an3.100:15.7: Meanwhile, another individual does the same trivial bad deed, but experiences it in this life, so that not even a little remains, let alone a lot. …
translation_text.an3.100:18.2: ‘No matter how this person performs a deed, they experience it the same way.’ This being so, the spiritual life could not be lived, and there’d be no chance of making a complete end of suffering.
translation_text.an3.100:18.4: ‘No matter how this person performs a deed, they experience the result as it should be experienced.’ This being so, the spiritual life can be lived, and there is a chance of making a complete end of suffering.”

variant_text.an3.100:1.1: Yo → yo kho (sya-all, km); yo ca kho (mr)
variant_text.an3.100:4.1: loṇakapallaṁ → loṇaphalaṁ (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed) | udakamallake → udakakapallake (mr)
variant_text.an3.100:4.3: taṁ parittaṁ udakaṁ → udakamallake udakaṁ (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed)
variant_text.an3.100:4.12: loṇakapallena loṇo na assa apeyyo”ti → loṇaṁ nevassa appeyyoti (bj); loṇaphalena loṇo assa apeyyoti (sya-all, pts1ed)
